description: Trip planning module — handles end-to-end travel itinerary planning: flight search, hotel search, POI discovery, restaurant lookup, and multi-stop routing. Hard invariants gate planning until destination, travel dates, and origin airport are known. Soft invariants (activity preferences, budget, accommodation style) use a degradation heuristic: ask only when the trip is long enough that omitting them would materially degrade the itinerary.

invariants.description: Trip planning cannot begin until all three hard invariant fields are known: destination, travel date range, and departure airport/city. The assistant must ask targeted clarifying questions until these are resolved.
Hard safety gate — immediate escalation on harmful content.

standing_orders.description: Ask targeted questions to resolve missing hard invariant fields (destination, travel dates, departure airport). Ask only for what is missing — never re-ask for fields already provided.

glossary.definition: A day-by-day or segment-by-segment plan for a trip, including flights, accommodation, activities, and dining.
The departure airport or city the traveler is flying from. Expressed as an IATA code (e.g. SYR) or 'City, ST' format.
The date range of the trip — outbound departure date and return date. ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
What the traveler wants to do at the destination — e.g. beaches, history, nightlife, food, adventure sports. Informs POI and restaurant recommendations.
The traveler's preferred lodging category: hotel, hostel, airbnb, resort, or flexible (no preference).
